name: when-analyzing-user-intent-use-intent-analyzer
description: Advanced intent interpretation system using cognitive science principles and probabilistic intent mapping
capabilities: Analyzes user requests using probabilistic intent mapping with confidence scores
Breaks complex requests into components using first principles decomposition
Generates clarifying questions to resolve ambiguity through Socratic questioning
Creates actionable execution briefs with phased plans and agent assignments
Adapts questioning complexity based on detected user expertise level
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: All 158 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown files (SKILL.md, README.md, PROCESS.md, process-diagram.gv) with no executable code. All detected patterns (external commands, filesystem operations, environment access) are illustrative code examples within documentation showing how an intent analyzer would work, not actual implemented functionality. The skill describes a 5-phase methodology for understanding ambiguous user requests using cognitive science principles.
valueStatement: Unclear requests waste time and create frustration. This skill uses cognitive science and probabilistic reasoning to understand what users truly mean, ask clarifying questions, and create actionable plans with confidence scores.
limitations: Requires user participation for clarifying questions
Does not access external systems or APIs
Cannot execute code or modify files
Confidence threshold may require multiple clarification rounds

useCases.title: Clarify requirements
useCases.description: Transform vague stakeholder requests into precise technical specifications

useCases.title: Prevent miscommunication
useCases.description: Ensure accurate understanding before starting complex feature development

useCases.title: Improve task accuracy
useCases.description: Reduce errors by verifying understanding through probabilistic reasoning

promptTemplates.title: With Context
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this request with project context: [request]. Project type: [description]. Recent work: [summary]

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Part Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Decompose this multi-part request and identify dependencies: [complex request]

promptTemplates.title: Expert Mode
promptTemplates.prompt: Assume expert user. Analyze and generate execution brief: [technical request]

bestPractices: Provide project context like recent files and current working directory
Answer clarifying questions honestly for accurate probability mapping
Review interpretation before confirming to catch misunderstandings early
antiPatterns: Skipping clarification when confidence is below 0.8 threshold
Providing incomplete context that leads to wrong interpretation
Confirming understanding without reviewing the breakdown

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Claude, Codex, and Claude Code all support intent analysis workflows.

faq.question: How long does full analysis take?
faq.answer: Average duration is 15-25 minutes including clarification rounds.

faq.question: Can I integrate with other workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. Output feeds SPARC specification, feature development, and cascade orchestrator.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: All processing is local. No data is sent to external services.

faq.question: What if confidence stays low?
faq.answer: After 3 clarification rounds, request specific examples or suggest user reformulate.

faq.question: How is this different from standard clarification?
faq.answer: Uses probabilistic reasoning, evidence tracking, and cognitive science principles.
